description_long: This is a classic black rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust rectangular base with a glossy finish. The handset rests securely on top of the base, supported by a cradle that is integrated into the design. A coiled cord connects the handset to the left side of the base, providing flexibility for the user to lift and move the receiver. The rotary dial is prominently positioned on the front, with circular finger holes and white numbering for dialing. The rear of the base is smooth and slightly sloped, with the cord emerging from the back. The overall design is iconic of mid-20th-century telephones, often found in homes and offices.
description: A glossy black rotary dial corded telephone with a coiled cord and a classic rectangular base. The handset rests on a cradle, and the rotary dial is prominently displayed on the front.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the rotary dial, cradle, and coiled cord attached to the handset.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the width of the handset and the sloped sides of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth back of the base and the coiled cord extending outward.
description_view_3: Front-right view emphasizing the rotary dial, cradle, and the coiled cord's attachment to the base.
